100 Years Ago Today, This is What Was Happening ...

On March 24, 1926, the United States Senate began hearings on the controversial Fordney-McCumber Tariff, which could significantly impact American trade. In Europe, a strong earthquake struck parts of Italy, causing widespread damage and prompting fears of further aftershocks. Meanwhile, in the realm of aviation, the British pilot Sir Charles Kingsford Smith made headlines as he prepared for his historic trans-Pacific flight, showcasing the era's growing interest in long-distance air travel.
